# UI

<!-- Generated by /init — defines the target interface and UX preferences -->

## Interface Type

Native **iOS app**, scanner-first. The camera/scan view is the primary surface;
the user points the phone at a barcode and gets an immediate verdict. Secondary
surfaces: manual brand search, verdict detail (ownership chain), and settings.

## Target Platforms

- **iPhone only.** No iPad-optimized layout in scope (it may run scaled, but is
  not a design target).
- **iOS 16+** (see `STACK.md`).
- Portrait-first; the scan flow is designed for one-handed use at a store shelf.

## Framework

- **SwiftUI** (see `STACK.md`).

## Design Preferences

### Screen Layout (scanner-first)

The main screen is split so the camera and the result share it:

- **Camera viewfinder rectangle** (top ~44% of the screen) — a rounded box showing
  the live camera. Scanning is **restricted to this rectangle** via VisionKit's
  `regionOfInterest`; everything outside the viewfinder is dimmed. Scanning is
  **continuous** (point at one product after another). In the Simulator (no
  camera) the box becomes a manual barcode-entry field.
- **Display area** (the rest) — shows the latest verdict inline, or, when idle,
  instructions + the ODbL data attribution.
- **Online-lookup bar** (pinned at the bottom) — the persistent opt-in control for
  the online fallback (off by default, with a privacy note). See `FEATURES.md`.

### Theme System (extensible)

The verdict presentation is a **pluggable theme**, not hard-coded. v1 ships **two
themes**, and the system must be built so additional themes can be added later
without touching core logic (a theme is a view/style module fed the same verdict
model).

- **Minimal** (shipped) — bold & instant. The display area is **color-flooded**
  (red = Nestlé / green = no match) with a large icon, a one- or two-word headline
  ("NESTLÉ" / "NO NESTLÉ MATCH" / "NOT NESTLÉ"), the scanned **product/brand name**,
  the ownership chain (brand → parent), and a one-line explanation — readable in a
  fraction of a second at the shelf. Rendered inline beneath the live camera (not a
  full-screen takeover), so the user can keep scanning.
- **Informational** — calm & detailed. A neutral card showing the verdict *plus*
  the full ownership chain (product → brand → subsidiary → parent) and any
  supporting context, for users who want the "why."

The user picks the active theme in settings. Themes share a single verdict data
model so adding a third theme is purely additive.

### Appearance

- **Light and dark mode**, system-driven. Both fully supported.
- **Color palette: open for now.** No brand colors locked yet. Verdict colors
  (a "match" vs "clear" signal) will be chosen later and must remain
  colorblind-safe per the accessibility rules below (color always reinforced by
  icon + text).

## Accessibility

Hard requirements:

- **Full VoiceOver support** across all screens.
- **Dynamic Type** — text scales with the user's system setting; no clipped or
  fixed-size critical text.
- **Never rely on color alone** for the verdict. Every result pairs its color with
  an **icon + text label**, so the Yes/No/Unknown outcome is unambiguous for
  colorblind users and in bright outdoor light.
- Respect **Reduce Motion** and other system accessibility settings.

## Notes

- Two extensibility axes now run through the product: the **target company**
  (data-driven, see `PURPOSE.md`/`STACK.md`) and the **presentation theme** (this
  file). Keep both configurable and decoupled from core scan/resolve logic.
